title: EU's regulation on Batteries and Waste Batteries
bodyHtml:
In a letter received from the Turkish Exporters Assembly (TİM), referring to the Ministry of Trade, it is stated that under the fourth omnibus legislative package (Omnibus IV) published by the European Commission on May 21, 2025, a proposal has been submitted to amend Regulation (EU) 2023/1542 of the European Union (EU) on Batteries and Waste Batteries.

The said proposal includes a postponement of the effective date of the due diligence rules introduced for battery producers and other economic operators placing batteries on the EU market—from August 18, 2025, to August 18, 2027.

With this proposal, the Commission aims to provide companies with additional time to comply with the new obligations and to allow third-party verification bodies, which are experiencing difficulties in the authorization process due to the lack of an accredited standard, more time for structuring.

Moreover, on June 19, 2025, the Council of the European Union adopted the Commission’s proposal without any amendments.

On the other hand, under the Battery Regulation adopted in 2023, producers are required to publicly report their due diligence policies aimed at reducing the environmental impact of batteries. To ensure more effective implementation of these obligations, the Commission plans to publish guidance documents one year before the obligations enter into force. Additionally, it is proposed that the reporting frequency be reduced from annually to once every three years. The European Parliament is expected to establish its negotiation position on the draft in the coming period.
